title: A Danish Couple’s Maverick African Research Finds Its Moment in RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policy
summary: The work of Peter Aaby and Christine Stabell Benn has long been controversial. Until Robert F. Kennedy Jr. became the U.S. health policy chief, most vaccine scientists tended to ignore it. That has changed.

title: Kids Keep Getting Stuck in Hospitals, Even After Being Cleared for Discharge
summary: Some children are healthy enough to leave the hospital after a medical stay but have no place to go. Across the country, the practice of allowing children to remain hospitalized “beyond medical necessity” has become a costly problem, and states have struggled to address the issue.

title: Trump Bought Stock in Drugmaker as His Government Boosted Its Obesity Drugs
summary: New ethics disclosures show the president invested in Eli Lilly and a company that manufactures injectable devices as his health agencies implemented policies that benefited them.

title: Journalists Unpack Latest on Vaccines, Vaping, and TrumpRx
summary: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national media recently to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.

title: Kennedy Swaps Vaccine Rhetoric for Story Time but Can’t Quite Change the Subject
summary: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

title: Minnesota Lawmaker Proposes Using Hospital Tax To Fill Charity Care Gap
summary: A Minnesota Star Tribune-KFF Health News investigation found charity care at hospitals in the state is offered at low and arbitrary levels, prompting Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison to say, “There is more work in front of us.”

title: FDA Blocked Melanoma Drug as Confusion Reigned Under Makary
summary: A third of patients in a clinical trial had tumors shrink while taking a genetically engineered treatment known as RP1.

title: Kennedy, Balancing MAHA and White House, Says He Won’t Run for President in 2028
summary: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is trying to address the interests of his MAHA supporters, who view him as their hope for the future, while being a good soldier in the eyes of the Trump White House, which has been stepping back from some of the movement’s core priorities.

title: License To Deliver: Some Midwives Break the Law To Assist With Home Births
summary: Some states bar professional midwives from attending home births if they don’t have a nursing license. Their advocates say laws to allow midwife licensing would make home birth safer and more accessible, plus help address a maternity care shortage.

title: Trump Demands Medicaid Data for Deportation. Some States Go a Step Further.
summary: Several states have required their health agencies to take on another job: verifying immigration status among Medicaid recipients and reporting them to the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. North Carolina is the latest to pass such a law, and experts expect more to follow.
